name: Foundational Patterns
description: Domain-agnostic classical DES models that serve as educational building blocks and validation benchmarks — M/M/1, job shop, inventory policies, batch-and-queue, and similar canonical patterns that appear across industries.

name: Queueing Theory
description: Cross-cutting theme for blueprints where the central subject matter is queueing behavior (arrival processes, service distributions, queue disciplines, closed-form solutions). Often paired with the Foundational Patterns domain for classical queueing models. Proposed by Seeder during the M/M/1 walkthrough — not part of the canonical top-level industry taxonomy but a natural sub-classification for foundational queueing models. Awaiting editorial review — see proposal.csv row 1.

name: Pedagogical Models
description: Cross-cutting theme for blueprints primarily intended as teaching or validation examples rather than decision-support tools. Blueprints in this domain have pedagogical scenarios (teach a concept, validate a tool) rather than operational scenarios (support a specific business decision). Proposed by Seeder during the M/M/1 walkthrough — awaiting editorial review. See proposal.csv row 2.
